rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TRACEY ANN JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/1/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The fifth amended petition was e-filed on 3/6/26. Notice mailed before 3/6/26 was defective. (2) The unfiled copy of the proof of service received by the assigned examiner, along with an unfiled copy of the fifth amended petition, does NOT appear to be a true and correct copy of the proof of service. The copy is incomplete at item 4 of the proof of service. (3) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) Paragraph 3 of the petition states that the administrator took no actions requiring notice under IAEA, but he sold the Prius. (5) Paragraph 23 and Exhibit B of the petition indicate that the statutory fee should be $3,275.38, but paragraph 5 of the prayer requests $3,299.38. (6) If an additional $600 in cash was discovered, it should have been reported in a Supplemental I&A, especially if the accounting was waived. (7) Because the accounting was waived, this fifth amended petition should have been just a final report, not a "final account." Deadline to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues is 5/22/26. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FLORLITA O. DAMASCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Neither a petition for final distribution nor a report of status with a petition for additional time to administer the estate has been filed yet, as ordered on 7/22/24 and as required by Probate Code Sect. 12200. Letters were not issued until 11/8/24, but the administrator has not yet filed an I&A. [Prob. C. Sect. 8800(b).] No petition to terminate for lack of assets has been filed either. [Probate Code Section 12251.] The four other heirs have a right to know that they may petition for an accounting. The initial petition never set forth how the decedent's siblings are the heirs; her parents and their dates of death were not set forth, as required by LRSF 14.18 C.3 and 14.18 D.1. The Court might wish to consider suspending or removing the administrator, in which case, the surety must be noticed, as sanctions could be imposed. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NEIL W. MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/1/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An AMENDMENT must be filed to request the $12,000 reserve and to set forth the specific assets to be distributed (number of shares of each stock and the net amount of cash) to each heir. [CRC 7.651(b)(4) and LRSF 14.35 I.6.a.] (2) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) AFTER the AMENDMENT is e-filed, notice of it must be served with the remote appearance instructions attached. A courtesy copy of (1) the AMENDMENT and (2) proof of service of notice of the petition and amendment must be received by the assigned examiner by 5/22/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF GERALD DERYAN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Refiled, Revised And Correct Petition To Contest Living Trust Of Gerald Deryan Probate Code 17200
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Probate Code 17203 Notice requirement not met. Petitioner must correctly file the Notice of Hearing [DE-120] without a coversheet. Every section of the form, both in the caption and the body, must be fully completed, including the hearing date, time, and department on the first page. The second page must show that a disinterested person handled the mailing, with their name, date, and signature clearly provided. Although there are proofs of service dated 10/9/25, 12/30/25, and 10/30/26, there is still no properly filed Notice of Hearing for this petition. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
